# Badge Component Documentation

A small indicator for counts, statuses, and notifications. Can be used standalone or positioned as an overlay on another element.

## Component API

### Props

- `count?: number` — Numeric count to display
- `max?: number` — Maximum count before showing "max+" (default: `99`)
- `variant?: 'default' | 'primary' | 'success' | 'warning' | 'error'` — Color variant (default: `'default'`)
- `dot?: boolean` — Show as a small dot without content (default: `false`)
- `children?: Snippet` — When provided, wraps the child and positions badge as an absolute overlay
- `class?: string` — Additional CSS classes

## DOM Structure

### Standalone badge

```html
<span data-badge data-variant="primary">5</span>
```

### Dot mode

```html
<span data-badge data-variant="success" data-dot></span>
```

### Wrapper mode (with children)

```html
<div data-badge-wrapper>
  <!-- slotted children -->
  <span data-badge data-variant="error">3</span>
</div>
```

## Usage Examples

### Standalone Count

```svelte
<script>
  import { Badge } from '@rokkit/ui'
</script>

<Badge count={5} />
<Badge count={5} variant="error" />
<Badge count={150} max={99} variant="error" /> <!-- renders "99+" -->
```

### Dot Indicator

```svelte
<Badge dot variant="success" />
<Badge dot variant="error" />
```

### Wrapping an Element

The badge positions absolutely in the top-right corner of the wrapped element:

```svelte
<Badge count={3} variant="error">
  <Button>Messages</Button>
</Badge>
```

## Styling Hooks

- `[data-badge]` — The badge element itself
- `[data-badge][data-variant='primary']` — Primary color variant
- `[data-badge][data-variant='success']` — Success color variant
- `[data-badge][data-variant='warning']` — Warning color variant
- `[data-badge][data-variant='error']` — Error color variant
- `[data-badge][data-dot]` — Dot mode (no text, smaller)
- `[data-badge-wrapper]` — Wrapper div when children are provided (sets `position: relative`)
